<color=orange><size=35>CAPTURING</size></color>
A pawn can only capture the opponent's piece on either of the two squares diagonally in the front, but cannot move to those squares if they are empty.

<color=orange><size=35>CAPTURING</size></color>
Capturing opponent's pieces is the main goal in chess. When a move you make lands on a square where the opponent's piece is standing, that piece is captured by you.

<color=orange><size=35>CAPTURING</size></color>
Now let's capture the opponent's Queen using your Pawn.

<color=orange><size=35>CASTLING</size></color>
Also you can not castle if the King is in check, moves into check or goes through a square that will put the King in check.

<color=orange><size=35>CASTLING</size></color>
Castling on the right side is called Kingside Castling. Let's move the King near the Rook to perform Castling.

<color=orange><size=35>CASTLING</size></color>
Now let's move the King two squares towards the left Rook to perform Queenside Castling.

<color=orange><size=35>CASTLING</size></color>
Once in every game, you can make a special move involving the King and one of the Rooks. It is called Castling. It is the only time in chess you can move two pieces in one move.

<color=orange><size=35>CASTLING</size></color>
Similarly, Castling on the left side is called Queenside Castling.

<color=orange><size=35>CASTLING</size></color>
The King and Rook involved in castling must not have previously moved and there are no pieces in the way.

<color=orange><size=35>CASTLING</size></color>
The King moves two squares towards any of the Rooks, and that Rook moves directly to the other side of the King. But there are certain conditions, continue to learn more.

<color=orange><size=35>CHECK</size></color>
A King is in Check when it is under attack by an enemy piece.

<color=orange><size=35>CHECK</size></color>
Now let's check the opponent's King by moving your Rook from d2 to f2.

<color=orange><size=35>CHECKMATE</size></color>
Black's King is hiding safely behind the pawns. Let's checkmate while also capturing the opponent's Knight by moving your Queen from d6 to f8.

<color=orange><size=35>CHECKMATE</size></color>
You win the game when your opponent is in check and cannot escape. It is called Checkmate!

<color=orange><size=35>En PASSANT</size></color>
It is a special Pawn capture move. If your opponent moves a Pawn two squares from its starting position, you can capture it with one of your Pawn as if it had moved just one square.

<color=orange><size=35>En PASSANT</size></color>
Opponent's Pawn is threatening your Queen on e4, let's make the En passant move by moving your Pawn diagonally from c5 to d6.

<color=orange><size=35>En PASSANT</size></color>
This move is possible on the very next move. Continue to see the opponent move its Pawn from d7 to d5.

<color=orange><size=35>GETTING OUT OF CHECK</size></color>
Black's Queen has your King in check, can you find the move to get out of check and go to a safe place?

<color=orange><size=35>GETTING OUT OF CHECK</size></color>
Move the King out of check, capture the checking piece, or block the check using another piece, unless that piece is blocking another check.

<color=orange><size=35>GETTING OUT OF CHECK</size></color>
The King is never captured in chess, so If you are in check, you must get out of check by using any of the following methods...

<color=orange><size=35>NOTATION</size></color>
Each square in chess is uniquely identified by a set of coordinates, a–h for the files (horizontal) followed by 1–8 for the ranks (vertical).

<color=orange><size=35>NOTATION</size></color>
Your Queen is currently on e3, let's move it to c5. Try to find c5 using the letters on the border.

<color=orange><size=35>PAWN PROMOTION</size></color>
Let's move the Pawn to the end, then you can choose any piece you like to replace the Pawn with.

<color=orange><size=35>PAWN PROMOTION</size></color>
When a Pawn reaches the end of the board, it can be replaced with any piece, except a King. This is called Pawn Promotion.

<color=orange><size=35>STALEMATE</size></color>
If the current player is not in check but has no legal move, it results in a Stalemate.

<color=orange><size=35>STALEMATE</size></color>
In the current situation, moving the Rook from g3 to g7 will result in a Stalemate.

<color=orange><size=35>STALEMATE</size></color>
The game ends as a draw and no one wins, even if the other player has many more pieces left.

<color=orange><size=35>THE BISHOP</size></color>
The Bishop moves any number of empty squares diagonally. It can not jump over other pieces.

<color=orange><size=35>THE BISHOP</size></color>
Try moving the Bishop to any of the legal squares.

<color=orange><size=35>THE KING</size></color>
Also a point to remember, the player cannot make any move involving any other piece that places their own King in check.

<color=orange><size=35>THE KING</size></color>
The King moves one empty square in any direction, unless the move would place the King in check. It can not jump over other pieces.

<color=orange><size=35>THE KING</size></color>
Try moving the King to any of the legal squares.

<color=orange><size=35>THE KNIGHT</size></color>
The Knight moves in an L-shape in any direction, first moving two squares in one direction and then one square sideways. It can jump over other pieces.

<color=orange><size=35>THE KNIGHT</size></color>
Try moving the Knight to any of the legal squares.

<color=orange><size=35>THE PAWN</size></color>
A Pawn can only move forward one square, if that square is empty. If it has not yet moved, a Pawn also has the option of moving two squares forward, if both squares are empty.

<color=orange><size=35>THE PAWN</size></color>
Tap on the Pawn's square, then tap on any of the indicated green squares to move, legal moves are indicated with a green square.

<color=orange><size=35>THE QUEEN</size></color>
The Queen is the most powerful piece, it moves any number of empty squares horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. Combining the moves of Rook and Bishop. It can not jump over other pieces.

<color=orange><size=35>THE QUEEN</size></color>
Try moving the Queen to any of the legal squares.

<color=orange><size=35>THE ROOK</size></color>
Tap on the Rook's square and try moving to any of the legal squares.

<color=orange><size=35>THE ROOK</size></color>
The Rook moves any number of empty squares horizontally or vertically. It can not jump over other pieces.
